meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage SaaS pricing strategies to overcome a lack of portfolio. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid delivery.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a high-value SaaS pricing calculator as your flagship project. By engineering a tool that solves a tangible business problem, you demonstrate technical authority, product-market fit understanding, and immediate value—turning a blank slate into a functional, revenue-generating asset that clients trust.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, a silent GitHub profile, and a client list that doesn't exist yet. The dreaded 'no portfolio' syndrome is the ultimate barrier to entry, keeping you trapped in low-tier bidding wars while you watch others ship elite products. You don't need years of legacy work; you need one piece of undeniable technical proof.

Today, we pivot. Instead of chasing imaginary projects, we are building a production-grade SaaS pricing engine. This isn't just a UI component; it's a strategic asset that proves you understand the intersection of code and cash. We will deploy this using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, transforming your lack of history into an indicator of modern, efficient development.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the core unit of value for your SaaS (e.g., tokens, storage, seats). This demonstrates you understand business logic, not just syntax.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing model that supports tiered subscriptions, usage-based billing, and annual discounts. Use TypeScript interfaces.

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Logic
steps.description: Build the backend logic that calculates costs dynamically. Precision here signals to clients that you take financial calculations seriously.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust calculation engine that handles prorated billing, tax estimation, and discount code application for a SaaS platform.
steps.claude_code_command: claude generate-logic --target pricing_engine.ts --rules 'handle edge cases for mid-month upgrades'

steps.title: Develop the React Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a responsive, high-converting pricing table. Clients judge your skill by the polish of the UI they interact with first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Tailwind-styled React pricing component with toggleable monthly/annual states and a 'most popular' highlight state.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect the frontend to a real payment processor. Showing Stripe integration immediately elevates your status from 'coder' to 'SaaS builder'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route for handling Stripe Checkout sessions based on selected plan IDs from the pricing component.

steps.title: Deploy and Portfolio-ize
steps.description: Wrap your code in a case study. Document the 'why' behind your technical choices to show you are a strategic partner.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that highlights the system architecture, the choice of stack, and the business impact of the pricing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write-docs --target README.md --focus 'system architecture and business impact'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for enterprise tiers; it makes your project look like a serious B2B tool.
Use mock data in your initial commits to show you can handle large datasets without performance degradation.
Avoid generic 'About' pages; create a 'Technical Deep Dive' page that explains your architectural decisions.
Add a toggle for 'Currency' support to show you understand internationalization early on.
Always link your live demo in your GitHub repo header; recruiters and clients rarely click through to find it.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your live link on LinkedIn or X with a thread explaining the specific business problem your pricing engine solves. Reach out to three small SaaS founders and offer a free audit of their current pricing page.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready asset that proves your worth to any client. Don't let this momentum stall; keep building, keep shipping, and keep iterating. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
